yesterdayAnalysis: Monday's brick workout was executed perfectly for taper week — 15.6 mile easy ride with no recorded power stress followed by a conservative 1-mile run at HR 137 (8 TSS). This was exactly the prescribed 40-min easy-moderate bike + 15-min brick run. The low intensity and volume are appropriate 12 days out from race day, maintaining touch without adding fatigue.
todayRecommendation: Execute the planned 2,000m pool swim with 4x100 at race pace to maintain swim sharpness, then enjoy Run Club as an easy social run. Keep effort conversational and ignore any temptation to push pace — this is about neural activation, not fitness building.
weekProgress: Week 12 of 13 is progressing on schedule with 27 TSS completed (14% of 200 target) through 2 days. This low percentage is completely normal and expected given the heavy back-loading toward Saturday's long ride and midweek brick sessions. All scheduled sessions have been completed as prescribed with appropriate taper intensity.
keyInsight: Taper execution is excellent — you're honoring the volume reduction while maintaining race-specific touches, exactly what's needed 12 days out from IM 70.3 Oregon.
positives: Sleep quality rebounded nicely with 78-score night after the July 4th disruption (41-score)
Perfect brick workout adherence — 13 completed through the build with yesterday's session maintaining the pattern
